big_picture:
• NDA hails Modi as longest serving continuously elected Prime Minister — 6 sources

• Fire at third-party data centre hits Google Cloud services in Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai — 3 sources

articles.title: Constitutional Pathology Of Delayed Adjudication: Reservation Without Preservation
articles.summary: Cessante ratione legis, cessat ipsa lex- when the reason for a law ceases, so does the law . Few maxims have travelled as quietly through the centuries, and fewer have aged with such inconvenient accuracy. It is among the boldest claims the common law has inherited, and for that reason among the most decorated and the least invoked. Treatises preserve it, judgments invoke it with deliberation, and its actual application has remained rare by design. To apply it is to assert that a law, though int

articles.summary: RBI/2026-27/108 DCM(Plg) No.S737/10.02.060/2026-27 June 09, 2026 The Chairman / Managing Director / Chief Executive Officer All Banks Madam/ Dear Sir, Withdrawal of old series of banknotes issued prior to 2005: Consolidation of instructions The Reserve Bank of India follows a policy of phasing out certain series of banknotes from time to time with a view to introduce banknotes with upgraded security features, in alignment with standard international practice of withdrawing old series banknotes.

articles.title: Delhi HC Upholds Prior Arrest Notice in GST Cases Since It Is Not Blanket Anticipatory Bail
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court held that directing GST authorities to provide seven days' prior notice before coercive action does not amount to blanket anticipatory bail. The ruling clarified that such protection merely preserves the individual's right to seek legal remedies while allowing investigations to continue unhindered. articles.title: Section 40A: Expenses or payments not deductible in certain circumstances

articles.summary: Fitch Ratings has reduced India's GDP growth projection to 6.4 percent for the current fiscal year. The US-Iran war is expected to slow economic activity in the September and December quarters. Rising prices will dampen consumer spending. For FY28, growth is expected to pick up to 6.7 percent. Fitch also anticipates the Reserve Bank of India will increase interest rates.

articles.title: Kalshi to collect employment info from customers trading in some high-risk markets
articles.summary: Kalshi will now assign a score to new markets, and the score will determine how much Kalshi believes that particular market is at risk of insider trading or market manipulation. If a market scores high on these metrics, Kalshi will require its customers to provide employment information to trade on that market. Those who Kalshi identify as a presumptive insider on that particular market will be banned from trading in those markets as they are created, the company said.

articles.title: India's biggest trade bet comes with six red flags

Similar issues are seen with banana imports.

Industry participants estimate illicit gold inflows could top 100 tonnes this year, hurting legal imports, refiners and government tax revenues.

articles.title: EU orders Meta to open WhatsApp to rival AI chatbots for free
articles.summary: The EU ordered Meta on Tuesday to give rival AI chatbots access to its WhatsApp platform for free within five working days as it carries out an antitrust probe, or risk a heavy fine. The EU had warned Meta it faced interim measures if it did not open WhatsApp to rival AI assistants in February.

articles.summary: Robots are learning to grasp objects with help from a new ultrasound wristband. Developed at MIT, this device captures human muscle and tendon movements. This data trains robots to perform tasks requiring fine hand control. The technology could enable humanoids to master housework and even assist in surgery.

Financially motivated cybercriminals also increased their targeting of tech firms.

The World Economic Forum report highlights how overcoming these challenges can drive economic growth and sustainability globally.

Exporters are blending tradition with technology to reach international markets.

articles.title: Indian economy, government finances, see mounting costs from Iran war
articles.summary: India's economy, once humming with benign inflation and steady growth, now faces mounting costs from the Iran war. As a major oil importer, disruptions to supply and elevated prices are impacting growth, inflation, and government finances. The central bank's ability to manage these shocks is increasingly difficult.

articles.title: El Niño clouds loom over kharif crops, farm pay
articles.summary: A developing El Niño event thousands of miles away poses a significant threat to India's agriculture. This weather phenomenon is linked to weaker monsoon rains and higher temperatures. Experts predict a potential decline in kharif crop production, impacting oilseeds and pulses. This could lead to rising inflation and reduced earnings for farmers across the country.

Notably, foreign investment in Indian debt has surged due to new government strategies incentivizing outside capital.
